summary: MDM4U continuous-distribution problems model data with the normal (bell) curve. Convert a value to a z-score z = (x−μ)/σ, then read the standard normal table for the probability; the 68–95–99.7 rule gives quick estimates.
formulas: 68% within μ ± σ, 95% within μ ± 2σ, 99.7% within μ ± 3σ
method: Note the mean μ and standard deviation σ of the normal model.
Convert the value of interest to a z-score: z = (x − μ)/σ.
Look up the cumulative probability P(Z < z) in the standard normal table.
For a 'greater than' use 1 − P(Z < z); for a range subtract two table values.
Use the 68–95–99.7 rule when the value is a whole number of σ from the mean.

example.title: Probability below a test score
given: scores are normal with μ = 70, σ = 10
steps: From the standard normal table, P(Z < 1.5) ≈ 0.9332.
